page: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 microarchitecture running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 16 vCPUs, offering a single thread per core, and includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and does not include GPU acceleration. Network performance is defined by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and compression performance. The instance achieves a top-tier passmark memory score of 4552.85 and a top-tier single-threaded gzip compression rate of 36.42 GB/sec. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth is strong at 178.83 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is average at 78.36 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 257.5 GB/sec. For database workloads, it demonstrates strong performance with 29240.13 database operations per second on Passmark and an average of 48.47 million Redis SET operations per second. In terms of compute, the stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores sit in the average tier, though the Passmark CPU physics test registers a strong score of 6145.24.

The m9g.4xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, where it achieves a strong throughput of 54.3 GB/sec, and lightweight machine learning tasks. For LLM inference using a small 135M model, it delivers a top-tier text generation speed of 376.71 tokens/second, though prompt processing remains average. Medium 7B model text generation is strong at 30.2 tokens/second. The absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for heavy storage-bound databases or large-scale deep learning training. However, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores offer reliable resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded application servers, microservices, and memory-sensitive utility workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It is configured with 64.0 GB of memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier memory scores and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong large-block memory read bandwidth and static web serving throughput. Compute and database operations perform in the average to strong tiers. The m9g.4xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded application servers, static web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
